g.data-narration: Welcome to Playfair Cipher - the first practical digraph substitution cipher invented in 1854.
The Playfair cipher encrypts pairs of letters called digraphs using a 5x5 keyword matrix, making it resistant to frequency analysis.
g.text: encrypts pairs of letters using a

g.data-narration: The Playfair cipher uses a 5x5 matrix combining letters I and J, encrypts digraphs, and was used in both World Wars.
Step 1: Choose a keyword like MONARCHY, then remove duplicate letters keeping only the first occurrence.
Fill the 5x5 matrix with keyword letters first, then remaining alphabet letters in order, combining I and J.
Before encryption, split plaintext into pairs. For HELLOWORLD, we get HE LL OW OR LD pairs.
If a pair has the same letter like LL, insert X between them. Also pad odd-length messages with X.
Encryption Rule 1: If both letters are in the same row, replace each with the letter to its right, wrapping around.
g.text: A to T (right), R wraps to A
Wrap around to first column if at the end of row

g.data-narration: Encryption Rule 2: If both letters are in the same column, replace each with the letter below it, wrapping to top.
g.text: M to C (down), U wraps to M
Wrap around to top row if at the bottom

g.data-narration: Rule 3: For letters in different rows and columns, form a rectangle and replace each with the corner in its own row.
g.text: H at row 2 col 2, E at row 3 col 1
